title: FLF: Domain Fronting CDN C2 Hunt
description: FreeLabFriday-inspired hunt for suspicious C2 candidates where non-browser processes repeatedly connect to CDN or cloud-fronting endpoints over HTTPS.
query: ('Log Source' = 'SOC Sysmon Network Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'Windows Sysmon Operational Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'SOC Windows Sysmon Logs') and 'Event ID' = '3' and 'Destination Port' = 443 and ('Destination Hostname' like '*cloudfront.net' or 'Destination Hostname' like '*azureedge.net' or 'Destination Hostname' like '*cloudflare*' or 'Destination Hostname' like '*workers.dev' or 'Destination Hostname' like '*graph.microsoft.com') and not (('Process Name' like '*\\chrome.exe' or 'Process Name' like '*\\msedge.exe' or 'Process Name' like '*\\firefox.exe' or 'Process Name' like '*\\iexplore.exe' or 'Process Name' like '*\\OneDrive.exe' or 'Process Name' like '*\\Teams.exe' or 'Process Name' like '*\\OUTLOOK.EXE')) | stats count as FrontingCandidates, unique('Destination Hostname') as CDNHosts by 'Host Name (Server)', 'Process Name', 'Destination IP' | sort -FrontingCandidates

ask_ai_prompts: Which endpoint process/CDN pair looks most like domain-fronted C2?
What additional TLS, proxy, or firewall evidence should be checked for SNI and Host-header mismatch?

references.name: Black Hills InfoSec FreeLabFriday Labs - Domain Fronting as C2

falsepositives: Developer tooling that uses cloud worker endpoints
